county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kenedy County formed 1921 from Hidalgo, Willacy. County seat is Sarita. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sarita. South Texas county created 1921 — one of the most recently created Texas counties. King Ranch territory (Kenedy County is almost entirely ranches). Very sparse population. Check Hidalgo and Willacy for pre-1921 records. Kenedy Ranch heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Kenedy County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1921; County Clerk in Sarita holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Kenedy County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1921 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Kenedy County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1921. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Kenedy County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1921. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Kenedy County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1921 to 1950 for Kenedy County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Kenedy County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Kenedy County from 1921. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kent County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Jayton. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Jayton. Rolling Plains county organized 1892. Kent County Historical Commission. Ranching heritage. Check Bexar for pre-organization records. Sparse population.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Kent County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Jayton holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kerr County formed 1856 from Bexar. County seat is Kerrville. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Kerrville. Hill Country county — center of the Texas Hill Country. Museum of Western Art. Kerrville Folk Festival heritage. Schreiner University archives. German settlement heritage. Check Bexar for pre-1856 records. Guadalupe River area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Kerr County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1856; County Clerk in Kerrville holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1856. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Kerr County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1856 to 1950 for Kerr County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Kerr County Area (Apache and Comanche)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Apache and Comanche in the Kerr County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Kerr County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Kerr County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Kerr County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Kerr County from 1856. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kimble County formed 1858 from Bexar. County seat is Junction. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Junction. Hill Country frontier county organized 1876. Kimble County Historical Commission. Ranching heritage (sheep, goat, cattle). Junction was a frontier outpost. Check Bexar for pre-1858 records. South Llano River area.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: King County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Guthrie. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Guthrie. West Texas county organized 1891. 6666 (Four Sixes) Ranch territory. One of Texas's least-populous counties. Check Bexar for pre-organization records. King County Historical Commission. Ranch records at Southwest Collection, Texas Tech.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for King County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Guthrie holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kinney County formed 1850 from Bexar. County seat is Brackettville. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Brackettville. Southwest Texas county. Fort Clark (frontier military post) — Seminole-Negro Indian Scouts stationed here. Kinney County Heritage Museum. Check Bexar for pre-1850 records. Border area with significant Mexican heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Kinney County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1850; County Clerk in Brackettville holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kleberg County formed 1913 from Nueces. County seat is Kingsville. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Kingsville. South Texas county created 1913. King Ranch headquarters in Kingsville — King Ranch Archives are essential for county history. South Texas Archives at Texas A&M University-Kingsville. Check Nueces for pre-1913 records. Strong Mexican-American heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Kleberg County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1913; County Clerk in Kingsville holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Knox County formed 1858 from Bexar, Fannin. County seat is Benjamin. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Benjamin. Rolling Plains county organized 1886. Knox County Historical Commission. Check Bexar and Fannin for pre-organization records. Ranching heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: La Salle County formed 1858 from Bexar, Webb. County seat is Cotulla. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Cotulla. South Texas brush country. La Salle County Historical Commission. Lyndon B. Johnson taught school in Cotulla (1928-1929). Check Bexar and Webb for pre-1858 records. Strong Mexican-American heritage. Ranching.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for La Salle County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Cotulla holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lamar County formed 1840 from Red River. County seat is Paris. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1916 courthouse fire destroyed many records.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Paris. 1916 courthouse fire destroyed many records — check TSLAC for backups. Northeast Texas county with significant African American history. Lamar County Historical Society. Paris Junior College archives. Check Red River for pre-1840 records. Significant Civil War and Reconstruction era records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Lamar County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1840; County Clerk in Paris holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Lamar County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1916 to present. Some gaps due to 1916 courthouse fire destroyed many records.

source_profiles.source_name: Lamar County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1916. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Lamar County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1916. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Lamar County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Lamar County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Lamar County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Lamar County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Lamar County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Lamar County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.
